title: Update StoredAuth for actor model
body:
## Summary

Update the `StoredAuth` type and related auth code to support actor-based authorization.

## Context

Transitioning from tenant-based to actor-based authorization. The stored auth token needs to include identity and effective tier information.

## Deliverables

### Updated Types
**File:** `internal/auth/types.go`

```go
type StoredAuth struct {
    Version       int       `json:"version"`  // Bump to 2
    AccessToken   string    `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken  string    `json:"refresh_token"`
    ExpiresAt     time.Time `json:"expires_at"`

    // Actor-based model (v2)
    IdentityID    string   `json:"identity_id"`
    GitHubActorID string   `json:"github_actor_id"`
    EffectiveTier string   `json:"effective_tier"`
    Entitlements  []string `json:"entitlements"`

    // User info
    Email    string `json:"email"`
    Username string `json:"username"`
}
```

### Migration

- Version 1 (old) tokens should continue to work during transition
- Client should handle both versions gracefully
- Consider re-authentication prompt for v1 tokens

### OIDC Client Updates
**File:** `internal/auth/oidc.go`

Update to handle new response format from platform including skip info:

```go
type OIDCResult struct {
    Auth   *StoredAuth
    Skip   *SkipInfo
}

type SkipInfo struct {
    Reason  string
    Message string
    Comment string  // Markdown for PR comment
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] StoredAuth v2 with actor fields
- [ ] Backward compatibility with v1 tokens
- [ ] OIDC client parses new response format
- [ ] Skip handling in OIDC flow
- [ ] Unit tests for version migration
- [ ] Unit tests for skip parsing

## Related Platform Issue

- delightfulhammers/platform#303 (Update OIDC exchange for actor lookup)
- delightfulhammers/platform#304 (Skip behavior with PR comments)

## Labels

- `auth`
- `phase-3`
- `actor-model`
